question: Compare Real Madrid and Manchester City Champions League performance over the last 3 seasons.
ground_truth: Real Madrid won the 2023-24 Champions League. Manchester City won 2022-23. Both have been semifinalists or better in most recent seasons.

context_precision.reasoning: Chunks show match results and stats for both teams over recent seasons, enabling comparison.

pipeline.answer:
# Real Madrid vs Manchester City: Champions League Performance Over the Last 3 Seasons

Over the past three seasons (2023–24 to 2025–26), Real Madrid and Manchester City have followed sharply divergent trajectories in the UEFA Champions League. Real Madrid won the tournament in 2023–24, while Manchester City's deepest run was the quarter-finals that same season, with both clubs experiencing earlier exits in subsequent campaigns.

## Season-by-Season Progression

### 2023–24 Season
| Club | Final Position | Group Stage | Knockout Highlights |
|------|---------------|-------------|-------------------|
| Real Madrid | **Champions** [2] | 6 wins, 0 losses (18 pts) [2] | Beat RB Leipzig 2-1 agg. (R16); drew Man City 3-3 away (QF) [1][2] |
| Manchester City | **Quarter-finals** [8] | 6 wins, 0 losses (18 pts) [9] | Beat Copenhagen 6-2 agg. (R16); eliminated by Real Madrid [8] |

- **Real Madrid** achieved a perfect group stage with wins including 4-2 vs Napoli and 3-0 vs Braga [1][2].
- **Manchester City** also swept their group, with wins over Red Star Belgrade, RB Leipzig, and Young Boys [8][9].

### 2024–25 Season
| Club | Final Position | League Phase Record | Key Results |
|------|---------------|-------------------|-------------|
| Real Madrid | **Quarter-finals** [7] | 5-0-3 (11th place) [4] | Beat Man City 6-3 agg. (play-off) [4] |
| Manchester City | **Knockout phase play-offs** [11] | 2-1-4 (22nd place) [11] | Lost to Real Madrid in play-offs; drew Inter 0-0 [12] |

- **Real Madrid** finished 11th in the new league phase format with wins over Dortmund (5-2) and Atalanta (3-2), but losses to Lille, AC Milan, and Liverpool [4][3].
- **Manchester City** struggled, losing 4-1 to Sporting CP and 2-0 to Juventus, and drawing with Inter and Feyenoord [12]. Their 22nd-place finish marked a dramatic decline [11].

### 2025–26 Season
| Club | Final Position | League Phase Results | Knockout Outcome |
|------|---------------|-------------------|-----------------|
| Real Madrid | **Advanced past Round of 32** (further round ongoing) | 4-0-2 (partial) [5] | Beat AS Monaco 6-1 (R32 1st leg); beat Benfica 3-1 agg. [5] |
| Manchester City | **Round of 16** [14] | 4-1-1 (partial) [13] | Lost 0-3 away to Real Madrid (R16 2nd leg) [14] |

- **Real Madrid** recorded notable wins over Juventus (1-0) and Marseille (2-1), but lost 1-2 to Manchester City at home [5].
- **Manchester City** had a strong league phase including a 2-1 win away at Real Madrid [13], but their campaign ended with their biggest defeat of the season (0-3 at Real Madrid) [14].

## Head-to-Head Encounters

Over these three seasons, the clubs met four times in the Champions League:

| Date | Stage | Result |
|------|-------|--------|
| Apr 2024 | QF 1st leg | Real Madrid 3-3 Manchester City [1] |
| Feb 2025 | Play-off 2nd leg | Manchester City 2-3 Real Madrid [4] |
| Feb 2025 | Play-off 1st leg | Real Madrid 3-1 Manchester City [4] |
| Dec 2025 | League phase | Real Madrid 1-2 Manchester City [5][13] |
| Mar 2026 | R16 2nd leg | Real Madrid 3-0 Manchester City [14] |

Overall, **Real Madrid won 3 of the 5 meetings**, with 1 draw and 1 Manchester City win.

## Key Takeaways

- **Real Madrid won the Champions League in 2023–24** and reached at least the quarter-finals in all three seasons, showing consistent elite performance [2][7].
- **Manchester City regressed sharply**, from quarter-finals (2023–24) to play-off elimination (2024–25) to Round of 16 exit (2025–26) [8][11][14].
- **Head-to-head dominance:** Real Madrid eliminated Manchester City in two of three seasons (2023–24 and 2024–25) and defeated them heavily in 2025–26 [1][4][5][14].
- **Manchester City's worst defeat in a season** came against Real Madrid (0-3 away in 2025–26), underscoring the gap in high-stakes matches [14].
- **Both clubs experienced the new league-phase format** (2024–25 onward), but Real Madrid adapted better, qualifying comfortably while City barely scraped into the play-offs [4][11].

The 2023–24 season was Real Madrid Club de Fútbol's 120th season in existence and the club's 93rd consecutive season in the top flight of Spanish football. In addition to the domestic league, Real Madrid participated in this season's editions of the Copa del Rey, the Supercopa de España and the UEFA Champions League.

The 2024–25 season was Real Madrid Club de Fútbol's 121st season in existence and the club's 94th consecutive season in the top flight of Spanish football. In addition to the domestic league, Real Madrid participated in this season's editions of the Copa del Rey, the Supercopa de España and the UEFA Champions League.
As the reigning European champions, Real Madrid contested the UEFA Super Cup against Atalanta, winning 2–0 and claiming a record-breaking sixth title,[1] then went on to win the inaugural edition of the FIFA Intercontinental Cup, beating Pachuca 3–0 in the final,[2] and also competed in the FIFA Club World Cup where they reached the semi-finals.

The 2025–26 season is the 131st season in the existence of Manchester City Football Club and their 24th consecutive season in the topflight of English football. In addition to the domestic league, Manchester City is also participating in the FA Cup, EFL Cup and UEFA Champions League, entering the latter for the 15th consecutive season.
This is the club's first season since 2014–15 without midfielder Kevin De Bruyne, who departed for Napoli following the expiration of his contract, and the first season since 2016–17 without Kyle Walker (to Burnley) and Ederson (to Fenerbahçe).
